text: Honestly been checking my portfolio way more than I should these days with all the geopolitical stuff going on. The swings are wild - one day we're up on peace talks rumors, next day it tanks on something else. I've started just unfollowing the financial news between my quarterly check-ins because obsessing over daily movement was stressing me out way more than it helped. I think what helps is knowing what you actually believe in long term vs what's just noise. When you see people freaking out about tomorrow's headlines, it's easier to just... not? Like yeah some positions get hit but if you're in solid companies with real fundamentals, the chaos kind of becomes an opportunity to add if anything. Also rotating some cash into stable stuff this quarter after running hot the last few. Not scared exactly just being more cautious with how messy things look right now.
label: neutral
